body.title: [sprint] Deploy AiderDevice across the swarm — 1-2 CPU-gated instances per laptop (4 boxes = up to 8 builders)
body.description:
**Problem:** Once AiderDevice exists (T-aider-rack-device-wrapper), the swarm spin-up that was gated on 'a builder that can reliably do it' is unblocked — aider IS that reliable builder. Deploy it across the 4 laptops so Granny has a pool of builders + CC.

**Proposed shape:** Deploy one AiderDevice instance per laptop (4 boxes), with a per-box CPU gate that allows a 2nd instance when headroom exists (Akien: ~2/box likely safe — VERIFY by watching CPU under real load, do not assume). Each instance registers with Granny (role=builder, instance identity per box). Inference routes to Hex ($0) or Ollama Cloud (watch free-tier burn — project_ds_cost_constraint). Laptops are orchestration SHELLS; the model runs on Hex/Cloud, so laptop CPU gates aider's local orchestration + repo-map cost, not inference.

**Affected files:** device config profiles per box; Granny worker-pool registration; a CPU-gate in the shim (concurrent-instance semaphore per box). Deployment scripts.
**Design rules:** worker-instance-identity per box; Granny handshake not spawn; watch CPU (empirical, not assumed); no SQLite.
**Scope boundary:** deploy + CPU-gate + Granny pool registration + confirm builders receive and complete dispatched tickets. NOT the device itself (T-aider-rack-device-wrapper) and NOT the corpus proxy endpoint.
**Completion criteria:**
- >=1 AiderDevice instance live on each of the 4 laptops, registered with Granny as role=builder.
- Per-box CPU gate empirically tuned (measured, e.g. 1 vs 2 instances) — the 2/box hypothesis confirmed or corrected with CPU data.
- Granny dispatches a real ticket to a swarm builder and it completes end-to-end (validated close).
**Test plan:** behavioral — Granny routes a ticket to a laptop AiderDevice, it clones/edits/tests/branches, automated gate + CC spot-check pass; CPU logged under load to set the per-box instance cap.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

## Escalation summary (Aider)
**What was tried:** I need to analyze the task and identify which files are most likely to need     
changes. Based on the requirements, I need to implement deployment of           
AiderDevice instances across 4 laptops with CPU gating, Granny registration, and
proper configuration.                                                           

The key areas that need modification are:                                       

 1 Device configuration profiles per box                                        
 2 Granny wo...
**Where it broke:** aider produced 0 edits
**What now?**
body.intention: I intend that the swarm becomes a pool of reliable aider builders (up to ~8 across 4 laptops) that Granny dispatches to — the swarm spin-up that waited on a builder that could do it, now unblocked by aider. Serves the factory-of-factories throughput goal.
